+reason = "Updating a record on a table with an index whose column is `id.<field>` (a component of a complex record id) must keep the index in sync. Before Codex finding 9c442c96 was fixed, `id.val` resolution on a complex id ran SELECT * against the already-stored new record while computing both `o` and `n` during `store_index_data`, so `o != n` was false and the old `id.val` entries were never removed — leaving the index pointing at the pre-update component."
+-- The index is on a component of the complex record id, not on a
+DEFINE INDEX idx_val ON tenanted COLUMNS id.val;
+-- WITH NOINDEX forces a table scan that re-evaluates `id.val` against
+-- each stored record. This is the "ground truth" the index must agree
+-- with. Before the fix, `id.val` on a complex id went through a
+-- permission-less SELECT * back to the same record, returning the
+-- doc-level `val` (none here, since we never wrote one) instead of the
+-- id key component — so the table scan itself disagreed with what the
+SELECT VALUE id FROM tenanted WITH NOINDEX WHERE id.val = 'x';
+SELECT VALUE id FROM tenanted WITH NOINDEX WHERE id.val = 'y';
+auth = { namespace = "test", database = "test", access = "user", rid = "tenanted:{ tenant: 'b', key: 2 }" }

+reason = "Codex finding c67c7232: `PERMISSIONS WHERE id.tenant = $auth.id.tenant` must check the immutable record-id `tenant` component, not the mutable document field of the same name. The first row's stored `tenant` field is spoofed to 'b' but its actual id-key tenant is 'a', so tenant-'b' user must NOT see it."
+SELECT VALUE id FROM tenanted ORDER BY id;

+reason = "Setup: a multi-tenant table whose permission compares `id.tenant` to `$auth.id.tenant`. Two records, both signed in as a tenant 'b' record user. Codex finding c67c7232: `id.tenant` must reference the immutable record-id component, NOT a mutable document field of the same name — otherwise the row's stored `tenant` field could be spoofed to grant cross-tenant access."
+DEFINE ACCESS user ON DATABASE TYPE RECORD
+-- notes:{ tenant: 'a', key: 1 } has a stored document field `tenant` of 'b'
+-- (a spoof). Without the fix, the SELECT permission compares the doc-level
+-- `tenant` field (== 'b') with `$auth.id.tenant` (== 'b' for tenant b user)
+-- and lets tenant 'b' read tenant 'a' data.

-reason = "Computed fields that dereference $this.id must not infinite-loop via document re-fetch. Covers object-keyed, string-keyed, and cross-record cases to verify cycle detection only activates for self-referential fetches."

+reason = "Computed fields that dereference $this.id must not infinite-loop via document re-fetch. Covers object-keyed, string-keyed, and cross-record cases to verify cycle detection only activates for self-referential fetches. Object-keyed `id.<field>` resolves directly off the RecordId key component (no document fetch), so cycle detection never fires for those paths; string- and array-keyed ids still fall through to the cycle-protected document re-fetch."
-value = "[{ id: test7:{ a: { b: 99 } }, nested: NONE }]"

+--    document fetch, no cycle detection involved), so foo = 1.
